title: Redox processes
summary: 9701 AS redox ‚ÄĒ oxidation numbers, half-equations, and balancing redox with PhET Balancing Chemical Equations.
learningObjectives: Assign oxidation numbers using standard rules.
Identify oxidation and reduction in redox reactions (OIL RIG).
Balance redox equations including ion-electron method where required.

simpleExplanation.title: Electron bookkeeping
simpleExplanation.summary: Redox tracks electron transfer. Oxidation numbers help you see whether a species loses or gains electrons even when no free electrons appear in the equation.
steps: Assign oxidation numbers: free element = 0, monatomic ion = charge, O usually ‚ąí2, H usually +1.
Increase in oxidation number ‚Üí oxidation (electron loss). Decrease ‚Üí reduction.
Write half-equations balancing atoms and charge with electrons.
Combine half-equations so electrons lost = electrons gained.

flashcards.back: Oxidation Is Loss; Reduction Is Gain (of electrons).

flashcards.front: Oxidation number of O in most compounds?

sections.content: **Redox** underpins electrochemistry, Group 17 halogen reactions, and transition metal chemistry. Cambridge frequently combines **oxidation number** assignment with **half-equation** construction.

sections.question: Balance: MnO‚āĄ‚ĀĽ + Fe¬≤‚Āļ + H‚Āļ ‚Üí Mn¬≤‚Āļ + Fe¬≥‚Āļ + H‚āāO
sections.solution:
Reduction: MnO‚āĄ‚ĀĽ + 8H‚Āļ + 5e‚ĀĽ ‚Üí Mn¬≤‚Āļ + 4H‚āāO

Oxidation: Fe¬≤‚Āļ ‚Üí Fe¬≥‚Āļ + e‚ĀĽ

Combine (√ó5 for Fe): **MnO‚āĄ‚ĀĽ + 5Fe¬≤‚Āļ + 8H‚Āļ ‚Üí Mn¬≤‚Āļ + 5Fe¬≥‚Āļ + 4H‚āāO**

sections.content: In acidic conditions use **H‚Āļ** and **H‚āāO** to balance O and H; in alkaline use **OH‚ĀĽ** and **H‚āāO**. State which medium you assume if the question is silent.
